title: Roll-bonded Al cold plate replaces brazed multi-channel plate
description: [800V Battery] Switch the pack-floor cooling plate from a vacuum-brazed multi-tube design to a roll-bonded 3003 aluminium plate with inflated channels. Eliminates ~40 brazed joints and a furnace cycle, cutting leak-test scrap while holding cell base ΔT under 5 K during 800V 350kW charging. Lower tooling and BOM cost per pack with no loss of fast-charge headroom.

ideaData.title: Roll-bonded Al cold plate replaces brazed multi-channel plate
ideaData.technicalDescription: BEV applicability: 800-V battery. Baseline is a vacuum-brazed plate with stamped 3003 sheet over extruded 6063 tubes, ~40 brazed joints and a 600 C furnace cycle. The change is a roll-bonded plate: two 3003-O sheets printed with graphite-pattern channels, hot-rolled, then hydro-inflated to form coolant passages. At 350 kW charging the pack rejects ~9 kW; channel design holds base heat flux near 1.2 W/cm2 with ΔT<5 K and pressure drop ~28 kPa at 12 L/min 50/50 glycol. Alternative materials: 3003 Al (formability), 1050A Al (conductivity), 6061-T6 (strength for structural floor), AlMn cladding for corrosion. Inflated geometry tunes local flow to hot cell rows.
ideaData.materialGrade: 3003 roll-bonded Al / 1050A Al / 6061-T6
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: Replaces vacuum brazing with roll-bonding plus hydro-inflation; alternative processes include CNC-machined manifolds or stamping+FSW. Removes the brazing furnace and flux clean, drops joint count from ~40 to near zero, simplifies leak test and cuts BOM. Stamping tooling amortises across platforms; assembly gains a single-piece plate.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: Eliminating brazed joints removes the dominant leak-test scrap and furnace cost driver.

ideaData.riskNotes: Roll-bonded plates carry lower burst margin than brazed tubes, so wall thickness and inflation pressure are validated to 3x peak coolant pressure for fatigue over 8k cycles; glycol corrosion mitigated by AlMn cladding. Fast-charge headroom preserved: base ΔT<5 K sustains 10-80% in ~22 min without cell derate. NVH neutral as flow rate unchanged. Thermal-runaway propagation margin unaffected since plate stiffness and contact area are matched.

evidenceSources.title: Taycan pack floor cooling plate teardown
Roll-bond cold plate process capability sheet

title: HPDC cold plate integrates manifold into pack tray
description: [800V Battery] Cast the lower cooling plate and coolant manifold as one AlSi10MnMg HPDC structural tray, removing welded inlet/outlet headers and separate plate. Function integration cuts part count and sealing interfaces while embedding channels directly under modules, sustaining cell ΔT during 350kW charging. Reduces assembly labour and leak paths for a premium 800V pack.

ideaData.title: HPDC cold plate integrates manifold into pack tray
ideaData.technicalDescription: BEV applicability: 800-V battery. Baseline uses a stamped plate, a separate cast manifold and welded headers bolted to a structural tray. The change casts plate, manifold and tray bosses as one AlSi10MnMg high-pressure die casting with cores forming serpentine channels under each module. At 350 kW the pack dissipates ~9-11 kW; cast channels carry ~10 L/min holding ΔT<6 K and ~35 kPa drop. Alternative materials: AlSi10MnMg HPDC (castability+strength), AlSi7Mg0.3 (ductility for crash zones), Castasil-37 (weldable cast Al), 6082-T6 for any machined seal faces. Integrated paths remove header welds and O-ring stacks.
ideaData.materialGrade: AlSi10MnMg HPDC / AlSi7Mg0.3 / Castasil-37
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: Replaces stamping+welding with high-pressure die casting; alternatives are gravity casting or extrusion+FSW. Sand cores or salt cores form internal channels, removing welded headers and ~12 O-ring joints. Large die tooling is costly but consolidates 5-7 parts into one, cutting assembly stations, fixtures and leak-test interfaces. CNC only on seal faces.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: Function integration collapses plate, manifold and tray into one casting, removing sealing interfaces.

ideaData.riskNotes: Porosity from HPDC channels risks coolant micro-leaks, mitigated by vacuum-assisted casting and 2 bar helium leak test; salt-core channels validated for wall integrity. Thermal-runaway: integral tray adds bottom-cooling mass improving propagation resistance per GB 38031. Fast-charge ΔT<6 K preserves 10-80% time and avoids cell derate at 350 kW. Off-road high-load load cases checked for casting fatigue at mounting bosses. NVH improved by stiffer single casting.

evidenceSources.title: EQS structural pack tray casting analysis

title: Direct refrigerant pack cooling deletes chiller and coolant loop
description: [Refrigerant] Replace the secondary glycol loop and battery chiller with direct R744/R1234yf refrigerant evaporator plates bonded to modules. Removes chiller heat exchanger, coolant pump and expansion tank for the battery branch, cutting parts and pump energy while delivering rapid pulldown for 800V 350kW fast-charge thermal headroom on a premium pack.

ideaData.title: Direct refrigerant pack cooling deletes chiller and coolant loop
ideaData.technicalDescription: BEV applicability: 800-V battery / refrigerant. Baseline runs a chiller transferring heat from a glycol loop to the AC refrigerant, requiring pump, expansion tank and plate-fin chiller. The change routes refrigerant directly through extruded 6063-T5 micro-channel evaporator plates with an electronic expansion valve per module zone. Two-phase evaporation gives high heat-transfer coefficient (~3-5 kW/m2K), holding cell ΔT<4 K at 350 kW (~11 kW reject) with near-isothermal plate temperature. Alternatives: 6063-T5 extruded Al (micro-channel), 3003 brazed Al, Cu-Ni for refrigerant compatibility, AlSi10MnMg manifold blocks. EXV control tunes superheat.
ideaData.materialGrade: 6063-T5 extruded Al / Cu-Ni / AlSi10MnMg
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: Replaces brazed chiller and pumped loop with extruded micro-channel evaporator plates plus brazed manifolds; alternatives are roll-bonding or CNC manifolds. Deletes chiller, coolant pump, expansion tank and ~6 hoses, lowering BOM and assembly stations. Adds EXV and refrigerant joints needing brazed/flared seals and stringent leak test. Net part-count and tooling reduction.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: Removing the secondary loop deletes chiller, pump and expansion tank in one architecture change.

ideaData.riskNotes: Direct refrigerant raises dielectric and leak-safety stakes: any leak near cells is managed with sealed bonded plates, pressure relief and refrigerant sensing per ISO 6469. Two-phase ΔT<4 K gives best-in-class fast-charge headroom, sustaining 10-80% at 350 kW with minimal derate. Durability: refrigerant cleanliness and EXV reliability validated over 15-year life; vibration on plate brazes tested off-road. NVH: EXV tick and refrigerant flow noise damped. Premium charge speed preserved.

title: Cure-in-place gap filler replaces pre-cut TIM pads
description: [Thermal Interface] Swap die-cut silicone TIM pads between cells and cold plate for a dispensed two-part cure-in-place gap filler. Robotic dispensing fills tolerance gaps with consistent bond-line, lowering thermal resistance and removing pad inventory and manual placement. Improves cell-to-plate heat transfer supporting 350kW charge while cutting material waste and labour.

ideaData.title: Cure-in-place gap filler replaces pre-cut TIM pads
ideaData.technicalDescription: BEV applicability: 800-V battery / thermal interface. Baseline uses die-cut silicone gap pads (~3 W/mK) hand-placed with ~40% offcut scrap and variable compression. The change robotically dispenses a 2-part addition-cure silicone gap filler (3-4 W/mK) to a controlled bead, achieving 0.2-0.4 mm bond-line and filling ±0.3 mm flatness variation. Lower interfacial resistance drops cell-to-plate ΔT by ~2 K, easing 350 kW charge heat extraction. Alternatives: silicone gap-filler (dispensable), graphite TIM sheet (in-plane spreading), ceramic-filled AlN gap pad (high-k), PCM paraffin pad for transient peaks. Material chosen for rework and dielectric strength.
ideaData.materialGrade: Dispensable silicone gap-filler 3.5 W/mK / AlN-filled pad / graphite TIM
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: Replaces die-cut pad placement with metered two-part dispensing; alternatives are screen-printed TIM or pre-applied PCM film. Eliminates pad nesting scrap (~40%) and manual placement labour, removing pad SKUs from logistics. Adds dispense cells and mix-ratio SPC. Bond-line control reduces rework; no tooling for die-cut pads. Leak/contamination risk low.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: Dispensing removes pad offcut scrap and stabilises bond-line for lower thermal resistance.

ideaData.riskNotes: Cure-in-place filler must avoid voids that create hot spots; vacuum-assisted dispense and X-ray sampling validate <2% voiding. Dielectric strength >15 kV/mm maintains pack isolation per ISO 6469. Lower ΔT improves 350 kW fast-charge headroom and 10-80% time. Durability: silicone resists glycol vapour and 1000h thermal cycling without pump-out; vibration bond integrity tested off-road. NVH neutral. Premium charge performance preserved through consistent contact.

title: Extruded micro-channel coolant manifold replaces machined block
description: [800V Battery] Replace a CNC-machined 6082-T6 coolant distribution block with an extruded 6063-T5 multi-port manifold cut to length. Near-net extrusion removes heavy material removal and chips, balancing flow to module plates within 5% while cutting cost and weight. Maintains uniform coolant supply for fast-charge thermal load on the 800V pack.

ideaData.title: Extruded micro-channel coolant manifold replaces machined block
ideaData.technicalDescription: BEV applicability: 800-V battery. Baseline is a solid 6082-T6 billet CNC-machined into a manifold with drilled cross-ports, ~65% material removed as chips. The change uses a 6063-T5 multi-void extrusion cut to length with stamped end caps and FSW closure, internal webs forming distribution ports. Flow balancing across 8 module feeds holds ±5% uniformity at 12 L/min, ~18 kPa drop, ensuring even cell ΔT during 350 kW charge. Alternatives: 6063-T5 extruded Al, 3003 brazed Al manifold, AlSi10MnMg cast block, 6082-T6 only for machined seal faces. Extrusion removes the chip-heavy machining route.
ideaData.materialGrade: 6063-T5 extruded Al / 3003 brazed Al / AlSi10MnMg
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: Replaces billet CNC machining with extrusion + cut-to-length + FSW end caps; alternatives are HPDC or brazed sheet manifolds. Cuts material buy-to-fly from ~3:1 to ~1.2:1, removes long machining cycles and chip handling. Adds extrusion die tooling amortised over volume. Fewer drilled cross-ports lowers leak paths and deburr labour.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: Near-net extrusion eliminates the bulk material removed in billet machining.

ideaData.riskNotes: Extruded manifolds must seal end caps reliably; FSW joints leak-tested to 2 bar and fatigue-validated over thermal cycles. Flow uniformity ±5% prevents cell ΔT spread that would cap 350 kW charge rate; CFD-tuned ports preserve fast-charge headroom. Glycol corrosion resisted by 6063 with inhibited coolant. Off-road vibration on end-cap welds validated. NVH: no flow whistle at rated drop. Premium pack uniformity maintained.

title: Single high-efficiency coolant pump replaces dual-pump battery loop
description: [Pumping] Consolidate two 400V-legacy coolant pumps into one high-head 800V-fed brushless pump with smart valve routing for the battery loop. Removes a pump, harness and controller while meeting flow during 350kW charge. Reduces parts, pump energy draw and NVH, improving range and lowering subsystem cost on the premium 800V pack.

ideaData.title: Single high-efficiency coolant pump replaces dual-pump battery loop
ideaData.technicalDescription: BEV applicability: 800-V battery / pumping. Baseline uses two 50 W pumps for redundancy and split flow paths. The change fits one 90 W high-efficiency EC pump (BLDC) delivering ~16 L/min at 60 kPa, with a rotary multi-port valve directing flow to battery, chiller or radiator branches. During 350 kW charge the single pump sustains required flow for ~11 kW reject at ΔT<6 K. Alternatives for impeller/housing: PPS-GF40 (chemical resistance), PA66-GF35, AlSi10MnMg housing, EPDM seals for glycol. Smart control modulates speed to load, cutting average draw ~30%.
ideaData.materialGrade: PPS-GF40 impeller / PA66-GF35 housing / AlSi10MnMg
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: Replaces two injection-moulded pump assemblies with one higher-capacity pump plus a multi-port valve; alternative is a fixed-orifice manifold. Removes one pump, its ECU, harness and mounts, cutting BOM and assembly. Valve adds one actuator but nets fewer parts and connectors. Tooling consolidated to one pump family across platforms.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: Pump consolidation removes a full pump assembly, harness and controller.

ideaData.riskNotes: Single pump removes redundancy, so reliability is upgraded with dual-winding BLDC and degraded-mode flow path; failure does not strand fast-charge thermal limits below safe derate. Flow at 350 kW validated for ΔT<6 K, preserving 10-80% time. Glycol-compatible PPS/EPDM resist 15-year corrosion. NVH improved by one variable-speed pump tuned below cabin audibility and resonance. Off-road sustained-load flow margin confirmed. Premium charge headroom retained.

title: Stamped-and-FSW cold plate replaces brazed cell carrier plate
description: [800V Battery] Produce the module cooling plate by stamping two 3003 half-shells and friction-stir welding the perimeter and channel walls, replacing vacuum brazing. FSW gives void-free coolant channels without flux or furnace, cutting energy and scrap while holding cell ΔT for 350kW charge. Lowers per-plate cost and leak rate across the 800V pack.

ideaData.title: Stamped-and-FSW cold plate replaces brazed cell carrier plate
ideaData.technicalDescription: BEV applicability: 800-V battery. Baseline vacuum-brazes a stamped 3003 cover to a channel sheet, with furnace cycle and flux residue risk. The change stamps two 3003-H14 half-shells and joins them by friction-stir welding along channel ribs and perimeter, forming sealed passages. FSW produces fine-grain, defect-low welds; channels carry 12 L/min at ~25 kPa holding base ΔT<5 K at 350 kW (~9 kW). Alternatives: 3003 Al (formable, weldable), 6061-T6 (strength), 1050A (conductivity), 5754 for marine-grade corrosion. FSW avoids melt, reducing distortion versus brazing.
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: Replaces vacuum brazing with stamping + friction-stir welding; alternatives are roll-bonding or laser welding. Removes brazing furnace, flux and post-clean, lowering energy and scrap; FSW tooling and fixtures added but reusable. Joint count concentrated in continuous welds reduces leak sites. Leak test simplified; assembly inline weld cell replaces batch furnace.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: FSW removes the furnace and flux while producing low-leak continuous joints.

ideaData.riskNotes: FSW weld integrity is critical; weld parameters and pin wear are SPC-monitored with 2 bar leak and dye-penetrant sampling. Void-free channels preserve flow for ΔT<5 K, protecting 350 kW charge headroom and 10-80% time. Corrosion: 3003/5754 with inhibited glycol validated 15 years. Off-road vibration fatigue on perimeter weld tested. NVH neutral. Thermal-runaway base-cooling contact area matched to baseline, preserving propagation margin and premium charge speed.

title: Graphite TIM sheet replaces ceramic-filled gap pad
description: [Thermal Interface] Use a compressible graphite TIM sheet between busbar/cell tabs and the cold plate edge in place of expensive ceramic-filled gap pads. High in-plane conductivity spreads heat from local hot spots during 350kW charge, lowering peak tab temperature while cutting TIM cost. Reduces material spend with no loss of dielectric integrity on the 800V pack.

ideaData.title: Graphite TIM sheet replaces ceramic-filled gap pad
ideaData.technicalDescription: BEV applicability: 800-V battery / thermal interface. Baseline uses ceramic AlN-filled silicone pads (~5 W/mK, costly) at tab-cooling interfaces. The change uses a coated graphite TIM sheet with ~400 W/mK in-plane and ~10 W/mK through-plane, dielectrically coated, spreading busbar I2R heat at 350 kW where tab heat flux peaks ~3 W/cm2. Lateral spreading drops local hot-spot ΔT ~4 K. Alternatives: graphite TIM (anisotropic spread), AlN ceramic pad (isotropic high-k), Al2O3-filled silicone (cheaper, lower-k), PCM paraffin for transient charge peaks. Coating preserves >10 kV/mm dielectric.
ideaData.materialGrade: Coated graphite TIM / AlN ceramic pad / Al2O3 silicone
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: Replaces ceramic-filled pad with die-cut coated graphite sheet; alternatives are dispensed filler or PCM film. Drops high-cost ceramic loading from BOM; die-cut tooling similar. Lower pad thickness eases module stack tolerance. Handling needs ESD/coating care. Logistics simplified by thinner SKU; assembly placement unchanged.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: Graphite's in-plane spreading lets a cheaper sheet outperform costly ceramic at hot spots.

ideaData.riskNotes: Graphite must stay electrically isolated; dielectric coating and edge sealing validated to >10 kV/mm to avoid busbar shorting per ISO 6469. In-plane spreading lowers tab peak temperature, protecting 350 kW charge from local derate. Durability: graphite resists pump-out and 1000h cycling; coating abrasion under vibration tested off-road. NVH neutral. Thermal-runaway: localised cooling of busbars reduces ignition risk, preserving premium charge capability.

title: PCM layer buffers fast-charge peaks reducing chiller size
description: [800V Battery] Add a paraffin/graphite phase-change layer between cells and cold plate to absorb transient heat during 800V 350kW charge, allowing a smaller chiller and compressor. PCM latent storage caps peak cell temperature, downsizing the refrigerant branch and pump while preserving 10-80% charge time. Cuts hardware cost on the premium pack thermal system.

ideaData.title: PCM layer buffers fast-charge peaks reducing chiller size
ideaData.technicalDescription: BEV applicability: 800-V battery. Baseline sizes the chiller/compressor for worst-case 350 kW charge heat (~11 kW peak). The change inserts a graphite-enhanced paraffin PCM (melt ~40-45 C, latent ~200 kJ/kg, k raised to ~5 W/mK by graphite matrix) between cells and plate. PCM absorbs ~30% of transient charge heat, letting the active chiller be downsized ~20% while peak cell temp stays <48 C. Alternatives: paraffin/expanded-graphite PCM, salt-hydrate PCM (higher latent, supercooling risk), PCM-impregnated foam, microencapsulated PCM in gap filler. Latent buffering decouples peak from steady-state cooling.
ideaData.materialGrade: Paraffin/expanded-graphite PCM / salt-hydrate PCM / microencapsulated PCM
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: Adds a PCM composite layer (compression-moulded graphite/paraffin) while downsizing chiller and compressor; alternatives are PCM-filled foam or microencapsulated PCM in gap filler. Smaller chiller and pump cut BOM and tooling; PCM layer adds one part and lamination step. Net subsystem simplification and lighter compressor mounting.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: Latent storage shaves the transient peak, enabling a smaller, cheaper active chiller.

ideaData.riskNotes: PCM melt point must sit below cell damage limit yet above ambient; selected ~42 C with graphite to avoid leakage and supercooling. Safety: encapsulation prevents paraffin flammability contact with cells, validated against thermal-runaway per GB 38031. Fast-charge: PCM holds <48 C through 10-80% at 350 kW with downsized chiller, no derate. Durability: 1000+ melt/freeze cycles without phase segregation. Off-road sustained load may saturate PCM, so active loop still covers steady state. NVH unaffected; premium charge preserved.

evidenceSources.title: PCM use in fast-charge BEV packs

title: Immersion cooling dielectric fluid for cell-to-cell thermal path
description: [800V Battery] Replace bottom-plate conduction cooling with single-phase dielectric immersion of cells, removing TIM, gap fillers and most cold-plate hardware. Direct fluid contact gives uniform cell temperature and exceptional 350kW charge headroom, deleting many interface parts. High development cost but strong part-count and thermal-uniformity gains on a premium 800V pack.

ideaData.title: Immersion cooling dielectric fluid for cell-to-cell thermal path
ideaData.technicalDescription: BEV applicability: 800-V battery. Baseline relies on bottom cold-plate conduction through TIM with cell-to-plate ΔT and top-of-cell hot spots. The change immerses cells in single-phase dielectric coolant (engineered ester/PAO) circulated through the module, contacting all cell surfaces. Direct contact gives heat-transfer coefficient ~500-900 W/m2K, holding whole-cell ΔT<3 K at 350 kW (~11 kW) with near-isothermal cells. Removes TIM, gap filler and most plate area. Alternatives for housing/seals: PA66-GF35, PPS-GF40, FKM seals, AlSi10MnMg manifolds; coolant options synthetic ester, PAO, or fluorinated dielectric. Uniformity boosts fast-charge and life.
ideaData.materialGrade: Dielectric ester/PAO fluid / PPS-GF40 housing / FKM seals
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: Replaces cold plate + TIM with sealed immersion module and dielectric pump loop; alternatives are hybrid bottom-plate plus spray. Deletes TIM pads, gap filler and machined plates, cutting many interface parts. Adds sealed enclosure, dielectric-rated pump and heat exchanger. Tooling shifts to sealed housings; leak test stringent. Net interface part reduction, higher fluid system content.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: Direct dielectric contact removes interface materials and equalises cell temperature.

ideaData.riskNotes: Immersion demands proven dielectric stability and seal integrity; FKM seals and enclosure validated against leakage and dielectric breakdown per ISO 6469 and UL. Isothermal cells give outstanding 350 kW headroom, fastest 10-80% with minimal derate, and improve cycle life. Safety: dielectric fluid suppresses thermal-runaway propagation, aiding GB 38031 compliance. Durability: fluid oxidation and pump wear over 15 years tested; fluid weight affects efficiency. NVH: pump and fluid sloshing damped. Premium charge clearly advanced.

evidenceSources.title: Immersion cooling for high-power BEV packs

title: Commonise cold plate across two 800V pack sizes
description: [800V Battery] Design one modular cold-plate cassette tiled to serve both standard and long-range 800V packs, replacing two bespoke plates. Shared tooling and part numbers cut piece cost and inventory while flow scaling preserves cell ΔT for 350kW charge in each variant. Commonisation lowers cost across the BEV platform without thermal compromise.

ideaData.title: Commonise cold plate across two 800V pack sizes
ideaData.technicalDescription: BEV applicability: 800-V battery. Baseline tools two unique brazed plates for two pack lengths. The change uses a tiled cold-plate cassette (repeatable 3003 brazed module-plate) with a shared inlet manifold; long-range pack adds tiles, standard pack uses fewer. Flow per tile is regulated so each holds ΔT<5 K at 350 kW regardless of count. Alternatives: 3003 brazed Al cassette, 6063 extruded tile, roll-bonded 1050A tile, AlSi10MnMg shared manifold. Modular tiling amortises one tool set across variants and future programmes.
ideaData.materialGrade: 3003 brazed Al cassette / 6063 extruded tile / 1050A roll-bond
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: Replaces two bespoke plate tools with one tiled cassette tool plus shared manifold; alternatives are scalable extrusion or roll-bond tiles. Single tool set across variants cuts tooling spend and piece cost via volume. Inventory SKUs drop from two plates to one tile family. Assembly fixtures common; leak test per tile standardised.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: One tiled plate family replaces multiple bespoke plates, pooling tooling and volume.

ideaData.riskNotes: Tiled flow must stay balanced as tile count changes; per-tile orifices validated so each variant holds ΔT<5 K and full 350 kW headroom. Shared part reliability proven across both duty cycles, including high-load off-road in the larger pack. Corrosion and fatigue identical to baseline 3003. NVH unchanged. Inter-tile seals leak-tested; thermal-runaway base contact preserved per GB 38031. Premium charge speed maintained in both variants.

evidenceSources.title: Platform commonisation in BEV thermal hardware

title: Integrated coolant valve module replaces discrete valves and hoses
description: [Combined Loop] Consolidate multiple discrete 3-way valves, check valves and hose junctions into one moulded multi-port coolant valve module for the battery/EDU/cabin loops. Reduces connections, leak points and assembly labour while smart routing optimises 350kW charge cooling. Cuts subsystem cost and warranty exposure on the integrated 800V thermal architecture.

ideaData.title: Integrated coolant valve module replaces discrete valves and hoses
ideaData.technicalDescription: BEV applicability: 800-V battery / combined loop. Baseline plumbs 4-6 separate coolant valves with many hose junctions and clamps. The change integrates them into one PA66-GF35 multi-port valve block with a single actuator cluster routing flow between battery, EDU, radiator, chiller and heater branches. Consolidated paths cut pressure-drop and ~14 hose joints; smart routing pre-cools battery for 350 kW charge (ΔT<6 K). Alternatives: PA66-GF35 (cost/strength), PPS-GF40 (chemical/temperature), POM for valve internals, AlSi10MnMg for high-pressure ports, EPDM seals. Integration slashes connection count.
ideaData.materialGrade: PA66-GF35 valve block / PPS-GF40 / AlSi10MnMg ports
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: Replaces discrete valves and hose tees with one injection-moulded multi-port module; alternatives are a cast manifold or 3D-printed prototype block. Removes ~14 hose joints, clamps and several valve housings, cutting BOM, leak paths and assembly time. Adds one complex mould tool, amortised over platform volume. Single actuator harness simplifies wiring.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: Valve and junction integration removes the dominant hose-joint leak and labour cost.

ideaData.riskNotes: Fewer joints lower leak/warranty risk but concentrate function, so the module is leak-tested to 2 bar and actuator redundancy ensures a safe default flow path. Smart routing pre-conditions the pack for 350 kW, protecting 10-80% time and avoiding derate. Glycol-resistant PA66/EPDM validated 15 years; off-road vibration on the block and ports fatigue-tested. NVH: valve actuation and flow noise damped below cabin audibility. Premium charge and cabin comfort preserved.

evidenceSources.title: EQE integrated coolant valve module teardown

title: Plate-fin chiller replaced by compact micro-channel heat exchanger
description: [Refrigerant] Swap the brazed plate-fin battery chiller for a compact aluminium micro-channel refrigerant-to-coolant heat exchanger. Higher area density cuts refrigerant charge, weight and material cost while sustaining heat rejection for 350kW charge pre-cool. Smaller package frees pack space and lowers cost on the 800V refrigerant branch.

ideaData.title: Plate-fin chiller replaced by compact micro-channel heat exchanger
ideaData.technicalDescription: BEV applicability: 800-V battery / refrigerant. Baseline uses a stainless or thick-Al plate-fin chiller with high refrigerant charge. The change uses a brazed 3003/4045-clad aluminium micro-channel core with folded fins giving high surface-area density (>1000 m2/m3), transferring ~11 kW at 350 kW pre-cool with lower approach temperature. Reduced internal volume cuts refrigerant charge ~20%. Alternatives: 3003/4045 brazed Al micro-channel, Cu-Ni tube (refrigerant durable), 6063 extruded ports, AlMn cladding for corrosion. Compactness lowers material mass and package size.
ideaData.materialGrade: 3003/4045 brazed Al micro-channel / Cu-Ni / 6063
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: Replaces plate-fin chiller with controlled-atmosphere-brazed micro-channel core; alternatives are extruded-tube or stainless plate exchangers. Lower Al mass and refrigerant charge cut material cost; brazing tooling similar. Compact core simplifies mounting and hose routing. Leak test by helium; fewer braze joints than plate-fin lowers scrap.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: Higher area density cuts metal mass and refrigerant charge for the same duty.

ideaData.riskNotes: Micro-channels risk fouling/blockage, mitigated by filtration and 350 kPa burst margin; corrosion managed by AlMn cladding and coating. Lower approach temperature sustains 350 kW pre-cool, protecting 10-80% time. Refrigerant charge cut also aids regulatory GWP exposure. Durability: pressure-cycle and vibration fatigue tested off-road. NVH: compact core reduces refrigerant flow noise. Premium fast-charge thermal headroom preserved.

title: Vapor chamber spreads module hot spots cutting cold-plate area
description: [800V Battery] Insert a thin copper vapor chamber between cells and coolant plate to spread localised charge hot spots, allowing a lower-cost smaller cold plate. Two-phase spreading equalises module surface temperature during 350kW charge, reducing plate metal and flow demand. Trims material and pumping cost on the premium 800V pack.

ideaData.title: Vapor chamber spreads module hot spots cutting cold-plate area
ideaData.technicalDescription: BEV applicability: 800-V battery. Baseline oversizes the cold plate and flow to handle local hot cells. The change adds a thin Cu/CuCr1Zr vapor chamber (sintered wick, water working fluid) between cells and a smaller plate, giving effective in-plane conductivity >5000 W/mK to flatten hot spots at 350 kW (tab flux ~3 W/cm2). Uniform spreading lets plate area and flow drop ~15% at ΔT<5 K. Alternatives: Cu vapor chamber, CuCr1Zr (strength), graphite heat-spreader sheet (no fluid), aluminium-water VC. Two-phase spreading decouples local flux from plate size.
ideaData.materialGrade: Cu vapor chamber / CuCr1Zr / graphite spreader
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: Adds a diffusion-bonded copper vapor chamber while shrinking the cold plate; alternatives are graphite spreaders or thicker Al plate. Vapor chamber needs wick sintering and sealing tooling, but enables a smaller, cheaper plate and lower pump duty. Net material cost can fall; assembly adds one bonded layer. Leak/charge test for VC required.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: Two-phase spreading flattens hot spots so a smaller plate and lower flow suffice.

ideaData.riskNotes: Vapor chamber dryout at high flux is the key risk; wick and charge validated to sustain 350 kW flux without dryout, holding ΔT<5 K. Sealing prevents working-fluid loss over 15 years; freeze tolerance to -40 C verified. Smaller plate must still give thermal-runaway base contact per GB 38031. Off-road sustained load checked against VC orientation limits. NVH neutral. Premium fast-charge uniformity improved, no derate.

evidenceSources.title: Copper vapor chamber dryout limit study

title: Cell-to-pack cooling deletes module-level plates and joints
description: [800V Battery] Adopt cell-to-pack architecture bonding cells directly onto one pack-level cold plate, eliminating module housings and per-module cooling plates. Fewer interfaces and joints cut part count and improve heat path for 350kW charge. Large complexity reduction and weight saving on the premium 800V pack, preserving fast-charge thermal headroom.

ideaData.title: Cell-to-pack cooling deletes module-level plates and joints
ideaData.technicalDescription: BEV applicability: 800-V battery. Baseline cools each module via its own plate, manifolds and inter-module hoses. The change bonds prismatic cells onto one pack-wide cold plate (cell-to-pack), removing module shells, per-module plates and inter-module coolant joints. Single shared plate with optimised channels rejects ~10 kW at 350 kW holding cell ΔT<5 K, with a shorter conduction path through structural adhesive/TIM. Alternatives: 3003 brazed Al pack plate, AlSi10MnMg cast plate-tray, 6082-T6 structural rails, dispensed gap filler bond. CTP removes a whole assembly layer.
ideaData.materialGrade: 3003 brazed Al pack plate / AlSi10MnMg / 6082-T6 rails
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: Replaces module-level plates and hoses with one pack-level plate plus structural bonding; alternatives are cell-to-chassis or cast tray-plate. Removes module housings, per-module manifolds and ~20 inter-module joints, cutting BOM, weight and assembly stations. Tooling consolidates to one large plate. Bond dispensing and cell placement automated; leak paths greatly reduced.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: Cell-to-pack deletes the module cooling layer and its interfaces wholesale.

ideaData.riskNotes: Bonding cells to the plate complicates serviceability and runaway containment, so adhesive selection and venting are validated against GB 38031 propagation. Direct path holds ΔT<5 K for 350 kW, preserving 10-80% time. Structural plate doubles as load path; off-road fatigue and crash validated. Corrosion and glycol resistance per baseline. NVH improved by stiffer one-piece structure. Premium charge headroom and pack stiffness maintained despite module deletion.

title: Model-based predictive precool control lowers chiller duty cycle
description: [Combined Loop] Add navigation- and charge-aware predictive thermal control that pre-cools the 800V pack before a 350kW session, flattening peak chiller load. Smarter software lets hardware run nearer optimum, enabling slightly smaller actuators and lower energy use. Software-led saving with minimal hardware change preserves fast-charge headroom on the premium pack.

ideaData.title: Model-based predictive precool control lowers chiller duty cycle
ideaData.technicalDescription: BEV applicability: 800-V battery / combined loop. Baseline reacts to cell temperature, so chiller and pump must cover unanticipated peaks. The change uses route data and charge-station prediction to pre-cool the pack to ~25 C before a 350 kW session, spreading the ~11 kW load over time and trimming peak compressor/pump speed. Lower peak duty allows downsized actuators and ~6% energy reduction. Alternatives in control: MPC with thermal RC model, rule-based predictive precool, Kalman cell-temp estimation; sensor options NTC vs digital I2C temp sensors. Software shifts load off the peak.
ideaData.materialGrade: Control software / NTC sensors / digital I2C temp sensors
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: Primarily a control change; alternative implementations are MPC firmware or lookup-table precool. Enables modest hardware downsizing (compressor, pump) without new tooling. Negligible BOM add (calibration, possibly added sensors). Validation via HIL and fleet calibration. No assembly impact; reduces warranty from thermal stress.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: Predictive precooling shifts load off the peak so smaller actuators and less energy suffice.

ideaData.riskNotes: Prediction errors must not leave the pack hot, so the controller falls back to reactive cooling with full margin, guaranteeing 350 kW readiness and 10-80% time. Predictive precool improves first-charge headroom and reduces cell thermal stress, aiding life and warranty. Safety unaffected; runaway limits unchanged per GB 38031. Off-road/unmapped routes default to conservative cooling. NVH improved by smoother, lower-peak pump/compressor operation. Premium charge performance preserved or enhanced.

title: Aluminium replaces copper busbar cooling interface bracket
description: [800V Battery] Replace copper cooling brackets that conduct busbar heat to the cold plate with weight- and cost-optimised 6061-T6 aluminium brackets with a thin plated contact. Lower density and price cut mass and material spend while keeping busbar temperature in check during 350kW charge. Material substitution saves cost on the 800V pack.

ideaData.title: Aluminium replaces copper busbar cooling interface bracket
ideaData.technicalDescription: BEV applicability: 800-V battery. Baseline uses solid copper brackets to sink high-current busbar I2R heat into the cold plate. The change uses 6061-T6 aluminium brackets with a Ni/Ag-plated interface and increased contact area to offset Al's lower conductivity (~237 vs 400 W/mK), holding busbar temperature <90 C at 350 kW peak current. Mass drops ~60% per bracket. Alternatives: 6061-T6 Al, AlSi10MnMg cast bracket, CuCr1Zr (where copper still needed), copper-clad aluminium for hybrid path. Geometry compensates conductivity gap while cutting cost and weight.
ideaData.materialGrade: 6061-T6 Al / AlSi10MnMg / copper-clad aluminium
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: Replaces machined copper bracket with extruded or cast aluminium plus plating; alternatives are HPDC or stamped Al. Cheaper, lighter material cuts BOM and freight; plating step added for contact and corrosion. Tooling for extrusion/casting amortised. Fewer grams reduce pack mass. Assembly and fastening unchanged.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: Aluminium substitution cuts both metal cost and mass versus copper brackets.

ideaData.riskNotes: Aluminium's lower conductivity and galvanic contact with copper busbars are the risks; plated interface and enlarged area keep busbar <90 C and prevent galvanic corrosion under glycol exposure. Fast-charge current at 350 kW validated for no thermal derate. Durability: bolted Al joint creep and fatigue tested over thermal cycles and off-road vibration. NVH neutral. Thermal-runaway busbar safety preserved. Premium charge current capability maintained.

evidenceSources.title: F-150 Lightning busbar cooling bracket teardown

title: Quick-connect coolant couplings replace clamped hose joints
description: [Pumping] Replace screw-clamped hose joints at pack coolant interfaces with push-to-connect quick couplings. Faster, error-proof assembly and lower leak rate cut warranty and labour while maintaining flow for 350kW charge cooling. Process improvement reduces assembly time and field leaks on the 800V thermal system.

ideaData.title: Quick-connect coolant couplings replace clamped hose joints
ideaData.technicalDescription: BEV applicability: 800-V battery / pumping. Baseline uses barbed fittings with worm-clamps, slow to fit and prone to under/over-torque leaks. The change uses push-to-connect quick couplings with integral seals and lock indicators at pack inlet/outlet and module junctions. Click-lock ensures consistent sealing and full flow (~16 L/min) for 350 kW cooling with no added pressure drop. Alternatives: PA66-GF35 coupling body, PPS-GF40 (chemical), AlSi10MnMg metal coupling for high-pressure, EPDM/FKM seal options. Tool-less connection error-proofs assembly and lowers leak rate.
ideaData.materialGrade: PA66-GF35 coupling / PPS-GF40 / AlSi10MnMg
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: Replaces clamped barb joints with moulded quick couplings; alternatives are SAE flange or threaded fittings. Removes clamps and torque operations, cutting assembly time and rework; coupling tooling added but reduces leak-test failures. Connection-verification feature lowers warranty leaks. Service/replacement simplified. Net assembly-cost and quality gain.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: Tool-less verified couplings cut assembly time and the leak-driven warranty cost.

ideaData.riskNotes: Quick couplings must not leak under pressure/vibration; couplings rated to 3 bar with lock verification and FKM seals validated over 15 years and off-road vibration. Full flow at 350 kW preserved with no pressure penalty, protecting fast-charge headroom. Connection-confirm feature prevents misassembly that could starve cooling. Corrosion and glycol resistance verified. NVH neutral. Thermal safety unchanged; premium charge performance maintained.

title: Shared battery-EDU coolant loop with smart valve routing
description: [Combined Loop] Merge separate battery and EDU coolant circuits into one shared loop with smart valve sequencing, removing a radiator, pump and hose set. Heat sharing pre-warms the pack and uses common hardware, cutting part count while protecting 350kW charge cooling via priority routing. Commonisation lowers cost on the 800V thermal architecture.

ideaData.title: Shared battery-EDU coolant loop with smart valve routing
ideaData.technicalDescription: BEV applicability: 800-V battery / 800-V EDU / combined loop. Baseline runs independent battery and EDU loops each with pump, radiator and reservoir. The change combines them into one loop with a multi-port valve sequencing flow: EDU waste heat warms the pack in cold conditions, and during 350 kW charge the valve prioritises chiller flow to the battery (ΔT<6 K). Removes one radiator, one pump and a hose set. Alternatives: shared 6063 Al hardlines, PA66-GF35 valve body, AlSi10MnMg pump housing, EPDM hoses. Loop merge pools hardware across two systems.
ideaData.materialGrade: Shared 6063 Al lines / PA66-GF35 valve / AlSi10MnMg
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: Replaces two coolant circuits with one shared loop plus a sequencing valve; alternatives are a brazed heat-exchanger link between loops. Deletes a radiator, pump, reservoir and hose set, cutting BOM and assembly. Adds valve and control. Common pump/radiator family across battery and EDU reduces tooling and SKUs. Leak test simplified by fewer circuits.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: Loop merging shares pumps and radiators across battery and EDU, deleting duplicates.

ideaData.riskNotes: Sharing a loop risks EDU heat reaching the pack during charge, so priority valve sequencing and a default-to-battery-cooling failsafe guarantee 350 kW headroom and 10-80% time. Cold-weather EDU heat sharing aids preconditioning. Reliability of the single pump upgraded with degraded-mode flow. Off-road sustained EDU load checked against battery cooling priority. Corrosion/glycol per baseline; NVH from one pump tuned low. Premium charge protected via control logic, runaway limits unchanged.

title: Fewer high-accuracy sensors via virtual cell-temperature estimation
description: [800V Battery] Reduce the number of physical pack temperature sensors by using a validated thermal model that estimates cell temperatures from a sparse sensor set. Fewer NTCs and connectors cut harness cost and failure points while preserving fast-charge thermal protection at 350kW. Sensor rationalisation lowers BOM on the premium 800V pack.

ideaData.title: Fewer high-accuracy sensors via virtual cell-temperature estimation
ideaData.technicalDescription: BEV applicability: 800-V battery. Baseline instruments many cells with NTC thermistors, each adding wiring and connector points. The change keeps a reduced sensor set at thermal-critical locations and uses a real-time RC thermal model (with current/voltage inputs) to estimate unmeasured cell temperatures within ±2 K, validated against full-instrumentation testing. Fewer sensors and connectors cut harness cost and leak/fault points while keeping 350 kW charge protection. Alternatives: NTC thermistors, digital I2C sensors, fibre-optic distributed sensing, model-only virtual sensing. Estimation replaces redundant hardware with software.
ideaData.materialGrade: NTC thermistors / digital I2C sensors / virtual model
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: Primarily reduces sensor BOM and harness; alternative is digital sensor multiplexing to cut wires. Removes thermistors, connectors and harness branches, lowering assembly and fault points. Adds model calibration and validation effort, no tooling. Simplifies harness routing and leak/seal points at sensor pass-throughs.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: Virtual estimation removes redundant physical sensors and their harness/connector cost.

ideaData.riskNotes: Under-sensing risks missing a hot cell, so the model's ±2 K accuracy is validated across duty cycles and a conservative margin plus retained critical sensors guarantee 350 kW charge protection and runaway detection per GB 38031. Fault tolerance: model cross-checks remaining sensors. Off-road and aged-cell drift accounted for in calibration. No NVH impact. Warranty improves with fewer connector failures. Premium fast-charge safety and headroom preserved.

title: Stamped serpentine channel plate replaces extruded-tube plate
description: [800V Battery] Replace an extruded-tube-on-sheet cold plate with a single stamped serpentine-channel 3003 plate closed by laser welding. Fewer parts and joints than the tube-array design lower cost and leak risk while a tuned serpentine path holds cell ΔT for 350kW charge. Process change reduces BOM and assembly on the 800V pack.

ideaData.title: Stamped serpentine channel plate replaces extruded-tube plate
ideaData.technicalDescription: BEV applicability: 800-V battery. Baseline bonds many extruded 6063 tubes onto a base sheet, requiring multiple joints and alignment. The change stamps a 3003-H14 sheet with a serpentine channel and laser-welds a flat cover, forming one continuous coolant path. The serpentine is CFD-tuned for uniform flow (±5%) at 12 L/min, ~30 kPa, holding base ΔT<5 K at 350 kW. Alternatives: 3003 stamped Al, 1050A (conductivity), 6061-T6 (strength), 5052 for formability. Single stamped channel replaces the multi-tube array, cutting joints.
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: Replaces tube-on-sheet brazing with stamping + laser welding; alternatives are roll-bonding or FSW. One stamped plate and one weld seam replace many tube joints, cutting BOM, alignment fixtures and leak paths. Stamping die tooling added, amortised over volume. Inline laser weld replaces batch braze; leak test simplified to one circuit.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: A single stamped channel replaces the multi-tube array, slashing joints and parts.

ideaData.riskNotes: Laser weld seam integrity governs leak risk; weld is SPC-controlled and 2 bar leak-tested with continuous monitoring. Serpentine flow uniformity holds ΔT<5 K, protecting 350 kW headroom and 10-80% time. Pressure drop checked against pump curve to avoid flow starvation. Corrosion: 3003 with inhibited glycol over 15 years. Off-road vibration fatigue on weld validated. NVH neutral. Thermal-runaway base contact preserved; premium charge speed maintained.

title: Lower-cost Al2O3 gap filler replaces AlN ceramic TIM in non-peak zones
description: [Thermal Interface] Use an Al2O3-filled silicone gap filler in lower-flux pack zones, reserving costly AlN ceramic TIM only for hot-spot regions. Zoned TIM specification matches conductivity to local heat flux, cutting expensive ceramic spend while keeping cell ΔT within limits during 350kW charge. Material rationalisation lowers TIM cost on the 800V pack.

ideaData.title: Lower-cost Al2O3 gap filler replaces AlN ceramic TIM in non-peak zones
ideaData.technicalDescription: BEV applicability: 800-V battery / thermal interface. Baseline applies a single high-cost AlN-filled TIM (~5-6 W/mK) across the whole module, over-specified for low-flux areas. The change zones the TIM: Al2O3-filled silicone (~2.5-3 W/mK, much cheaper) in cooler regions and AlN only where flux exceeds ~2 W/cm2 at 350 kW. Local conductivity matched to heat flux keeps cell ΔT within spec. Alternatives: Al2O3-filled silicone, AlN ceramic (hot spots), boron-nitride filled (mid-k), dispensable gap filler. Zoning spends premium material only where needed.
ideaData.materialGrade: Al2O3-filled silicone / AlN ceramic / BN-filled silicone
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: Replaces uniform AlN pad with zoned die-cut/dispensed TIM by region; alternatives are graded fillers or printed TIM. Cuts costly ceramic loading from most of the area; tooling for zoned die-cut or dual-dispense added. Assembly must place correct zone, controlled by tooling/poka-yoke. Lower-cost SKU dominates volume; logistics manage two grades.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: Matching TIM conductivity to local flux spends premium ceramic only at hot spots.

ideaData.riskNotes: Mis-zoning could under-cool a hot region, so flux maps from 350 kW charge thermal models define zones with margin and placement is poka-yoke'd. Cell ΔT validated within spec across zones, preserving fast-charge headroom and 10-80% time. Both fillers maintain dielectric >15 kV/mm per ISO 6469 and resist pump-out and 1000h cycling. Off-road vibration tested. NVH neutral. Premium charge thermal performance preserved with lower material cost.

evidenceSources.title: Al2O3 vs AlN gap filler cost/thermal comparison
Zoned TIM strategies in BEV packs

title: Brazed-Al replaces stainless coolant-to-coolant heat exchanger
description: [Combined Loop] Replace a stainless plate heat exchanger linking battery and chiller loops with a brazed-aluminium counterpart. Lower density and material cost cut weight and BOM while matched surface area sustains heat transfer for 350kW charge pre-cool. Material substitution lowers cost on the 800V combined thermal loop.

ideaData.title: Brazed-Al replaces stainless coolant-to-coolant heat exchanger
ideaData.technicalDescription: BEV applicability: 800-V battery / combined loop. Baseline uses a stainless-steel brazed plate heat exchanger between the battery glycol loop and the chiller loop, heavy and costly. The change uses a controlled-atmosphere-brazed 3003/4045 aluminium plate exchanger with matched plate count to hold the same ~11 kW transfer at 350 kW pre-cool, with low approach temperature. Aluminium cuts mass ~55% and cost. Alternatives: 3003/4045 brazed Al, 6063 ported Al, Cu-Ni (durability), AlMn-clad for corrosion. Material swap preserves duty at lower weight and price.
ideaData.materialGrade: 3003/4045 brazed Al / 6063 / Cu-Ni
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: Replaces stainless plate exchanger with brazed-Al plate stack; alternatives are micro-channel or tube-fin Al. Aluminium brazing tooling differs but material and mass cost drop. Compatible with Al hardlines reduces galvanic transition fittings. Helium leak test; fewer dissimilar-metal joints. Lighter unit eases mounting and assembly.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: Aluminium substitution cuts exchanger mass and cost at equal heat duty.

ideaData.riskNotes: Aluminium's lower strength and corrosion margin versus stainless are the risks; AlMn cladding, inhibited glycol and 3x burst-pressure design validated over 15 years and thermal cycling. Matched plate area sustains 350 kW pre-cool, protecting 10-80% time. Galvanic compatibility with Al loop avoids mixed-metal corrosion. Off-road vibration fatigue tested. NVH neutral. Thermal performance and premium fast-charge headroom preserved.

evidenceSources.title: Brazed-Al vs stainless HX cost/weight study

title: Integrated reservoir-pump-sensor thermal module cuts mounts and hoses
description: [Pumping] Combine the coolant reservoir, pump, level/temp sensors and a degas chamber into one integrated thermal management module, replacing discrete components and brackets. Fewer hoses, mounts and connectors cut assembly labour and leak points while maintaining flow for 350kW charge. Integration lowers cost on the 800V thermal system.

ideaData.title: Integrated reservoir-pump-sensor thermal module cuts mounts and hoses
ideaData.technicalDescription: BEV applicability: 800-V battery / pumping. Baseline mounts reservoir, pump, degas bottle and sensors separately with interconnecting hoses and brackets. The change integrates them into one PA66-GF35 module with internal flow paths, an EC pump, integral degassing and combined level/temperature sensing. Internal channels remove ~8 hose joints and several brackets while delivering ~16 L/min at 60 kPa for 350 kW cooling. Alternatives: PA66-GF35 housing, PPS-GF40 (temperature/chemical), AlSi10MnMg pump section, EPDM seals. Integration consolidates a cluster of parts into one module.
ideaData.materialGrade: PA66-GF35 module / PPS-GF40 / AlSi10MnMg pump section
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: Replaces discrete reservoir, pump, degas bottle and sensors with one moulded module; alternatives are a cast manifold-integrated pump. Removes ~8 hose joints, brackets and connectors, cutting BOM, leak paths and assembly stations. One complex mould tool amortised over volume. Integral degas improves fill/bleed. Single connector simplifies wiring and leak test.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: Integrating reservoir, pump and sensors removes hoses, mounts and connectors in one module.

ideaData.riskNotes: Concentrating functions raises single-point-failure stakes, so the pump uses dual-winding BLDC and the module is leak-tested to 2 bar with integral degas validated to prevent air-lock that could starve 350 kW cooling. Flow and NVH tuned: variable-speed pump kept below cabin audibility and resonance. Glycol-resistant PA66/EPDM validated 15 years; off-road vibration fatigue on the module tested. Thermal-runaway limits unchanged. Premium fast-charge headroom and reliability preserved.

title: Topology-optimised CNC cold-plate channels cut metal and pressure drop
description: [800V Battery] Apply topology optimisation to the cold-plate channel layout, machining only the metal needed and lowering pressure drop. Optimised flow paths cut plate mass and pump energy while concentrating cooling under hot cells for 350kW charge. Weight- and energy-led saving improves range and cost on the premium 800V pack.

ideaData.title: Topology-optimised CNC cold-plate channels cut metal and pressure drop
ideaData.technicalDescription: BEV applicability: 800-V battery. Baseline uses uniform straight channels with conservative wall thickness and high pressure drop. The change applies topology/flow optimisation to route wider channels under high-flux cells and thin walls elsewhere, CNC-machined or cast into 6082-T6/AlSi10MnMg. Optimised paths cut plate mass ~12% and pressure drop ~20% (to ~22 kPa at 12 L/min) while holding cell ΔT<5 K at 350 kW, lowering pump energy. Alternatives: 6082-T6 machined Al, AlSi10MnMg cast, 3003 brazed (optimised pattern), 6063 extruded base. Optimisation removes non-load, non-thermal metal.
ideaData.materialGrade: 6082-T6 machined Al / AlSi10MnMg cast / 3003 brazed
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: Replaces uniform machining with topology-optimised CNC or cast channels; alternatives are cast-in optimised cores or roll-bond patterns. Removes excess metal, cutting mass and material; machining time optimised by toolpath. For cast variant, core geometry follows optimisation. Tooling per method amortised. Leak test unchanged; lower pump duty reduces pump spec.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: Topology optimisation removes metal that carries neither load nor heat, cutting mass and drop.

ideaData.riskNotes: Thinned walls must retain burst and fatigue strength, validated to 3x coolant pressure and 8k cycles. Optimised flow concentrates cooling under hot cells, holding ΔT<5 K and protecting 350 kW headroom and 10-80% time. Lower pressure drop reduces pump energy, aiding range, with reduced flow noise improving NVH. Off-road vibration fatigue on thinned sections tested. Corrosion per baseline. Thermal-runaway base contact preserved; premium charge speed maintained.

title: Stamped roll-bonded inverter cold plate replaces machined billet
description: [800V EDU] SiC inverter cold plate moves from CNC-machined 6061 billet to roll-bonded stamped 3003 Al with inflated channels, cutting machining hours and leak paths while holding junction temperature on continuous high-load duty.

ideaData.title: Stamped roll-bonded inverter cold plate replaces machined billet
ideaData.technicalDescription: BEV applicability: 800-V EDU. Baseline is a CNC-machined 6061-T6 billet cold plate under the SiC power module dissipating ~12 kW at ~120 W/cm2 local heat flux. The change adopts roll-bonded 3003 Al sheet with internally inflated coolant channels, giving a serpentine path with hydraulic diameter ~3 mm, 9 L/min water-glycol (50/50), and ~28 kPa pressure drop while holding ΔT (junction-to-coolant) near 38 K. Alternative materials: 6061-T6/6082-T6 wrought Al, 3003 brazed Al, AlSi10MnMg HPDC, with AlN ceramic or graphite-foil TIM under the module to drop interface ΔT ~6 K versus silicone gap-filler.
ideaData.materialGrade: 3003 roll-bonded Al / AlN ceramic TIM / 6061-T6
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: Roll-bonding plus stamping replaces 5-axis CNC billet milling and vacuum brazing; alternatives are extrusion or HPDC. Cuts machined surface area ~60%, removes two brazed joints, lowers tooling amortisation and halves helium leak-test cycle time, reducing BOM and assembly station count.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: Eliminate billet machining via near-net roll-bonded forming.

ideaData.riskNotes: Roll-bonded channel wall thickness and burst margin verified to 3x peak coolant pressure; AlN TIM keeps SiC junction <175 C at 350 kW fast-charge and sustained off-road grade loads. Glycol corrosion mitigated by 3003 alloy passivation; fatigue validated over 15k thermal cycles. Pump-flow NVH unchanged at 9 L/min. Premium BEV performance preserved by holding junction-to-coolant ΔT within 2 K of billet baseline.

title: Direct ATF spray cooling of stator end-windings replaces jacket-only
description: [800V EDU] Adds targeted ATF spray bars onto stator end-windings, enabling higher continuous torque without upsizing the water jacket, improving copper utilisation and removing a separate end-winding potting heat path.

ideaData.title: Direct ATF spray cooling of stator end-windings replaces jacket-only
ideaData.technicalDescription: BEV applicability: 800-V EDU. Baseline relies on a peripheral water-glycol stator jacket where end-winding hotspots reach ~165 C at continuous load. The change sprays gearbox ATF (low-viscosity ATF, ~7 cSt at 40 C) through machined spray bars directly onto end-windings at ~4 L/min, removing ~3 kW localised loss and dropping copper hotspot ΔT ~25 K, allowing higher RMS current. Alternative materials: spray manifold in AlSi10MnMg HPDC vs 6082-T6 extrusion vs PA66-GF35 polymer; oil-to-coolant exchanger plates in 3003 brazed Al or CuNi for high-conductivity service.
ideaData.materialGrade: AlSi10MnMg HPDC / 3003 brazed Al / PA66-GF35
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: Integrating spray bars uses HPDC of the end-cap or CNC-opt of an extruded manifold; alternatives are additive (AM) spray-channel inserts or stamping. Removes separate potting compound step, adds one ATF feed joint, but deletes jacket up-sizing tooling. Net joint count neutral; leak-test extends to the oil circuit.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: Direct oil spray lifts continuous rating without jacket upsize.

ideaData.riskNotes: Direct ATF impingement holds end-winding insulation (grade 200 enamel) below 180 C limit during sustained off-road and trailer-tow loads; dielectric ATF avoids partial-discharge risk versus water. Churning losses managed by scavenge pump sizing. Fast-charge unaffected (motor idle). Fatigue of spray bars validated to 20k cycles. NVH: scavenge pump whine controlled with isolation. Premium BEV continuous-power performance preserved and uprated ~8%.

title: Shared pump/valve block couples battery and EDU coolant loops
description: [Combined Loop] Integrates battery and EDU coolant circuits into one machined valve block with a shared electric pump and rotary multi-way valve, deleting a redundant pump and harness while enabling waste-heat sharing.

ideaData.title: Shared pump/valve block couples battery and EDU coolant loops
ideaData.technicalDescription: BEV applicability: combined loop. Baseline runs separate battery and EDU water-glycol loops, each with its own pump and expansion path. The change consolidates into a single AlSi10MnMg HPDC valve block hosting a 6-port rotary valve and one ~25 W/m pump, routing 12 L/min where needed and recovering EDU waste heat (~4 kW) to warm the pack at low ambient, cutting PTC draw. Alternative materials: HPDC AlSi10MnMg vs 6082-T6 extruded manifold vs PA66-GF50 glass-filled polymer block; seals in EPDM vs FKM for glycol compatibility.
ideaData.materialGrade: AlSi10MnMg HPDC / 6082-T6 Al / PA66-GF50
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: Valve-block integration uses HPDC plus CNC-opt port finishing; alternatives are extrusion-and-weld or friction-stir-welded plates. Deletes one pump, ~1.8 m of hose, four hose clamps and two connectors; consolidates leak-test into one fixture. Tooling rises for the block but net BOM and assembly stations fall.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: One pump and valve block serves both thermal domains.

ideaData.riskNotes: Cross-contamination prevented by full hydraulic isolation between domains via valve sealing; pack thermal-runaway propagation path unaffected (no shared chamber). Waste-heat sharing improves winter range and fast-charge preconditioning (800V 350kW, 10-80% time reduced ~3 min). Single-pump redundancy mitigated by limp-home valve default. Glycol fatigue and EPDM seal durability validated 15-yr. NVH: one pump lowers acoustic sources. Premium performance preserved.

title: Pin-fin AM cold plate targets SiC die hotspots locally
description: [800V EDU] Additively manufactured conformal pin-fin cold plate places dense fins only under SiC die footprints, cutting coolant pressure drop and aluminium mass while lowering junction temperature at 800V.

ideaData.title: Pin-fin AM cold plate targets SiC die hotspots locally
ideaData.technicalDescription: BEV applicability: 800-V EDU. Baseline is a uniform skived-fin 6061 cold plate with ~35 kPa drop. The AM AlSi10Mg plate uses graded pin-fin density (0.6 mm pins) only beneath the six SiC dies at ~150 W/cm2, raising local heat-transfer coefficient to ~12 kW/m2K while cutting overall pressure drop to ~22 kPa at 9 L/min water-glycol, dropping junction-to-coolant ΔT ~8 K. Alternative materials: AlSi10Mg AM vs 6061-T6 skived vs CuCr1Zr inserts for peak-flux zones; TIM options AlN ceramic, sintered-silver, graphite foil.
ideaData.materialGrade: AlSi10Mg AM / CuCr1Zr insert / sintered-Ag TIM
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: Laser powder-bed AM replaces skiving plus brazing of fin stock; alternatives are die-cast pin-fin or stamped-and-brazed. Removes braze joint and base plate, enabling conformal channels impossible by machining. Tooling-free but per-part cycle cost higher; offsets via mass and pump-energy savings. Leak-test single fixture.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: Conformal fins cut both ΔT and pump pressure penalty.

ideaData.riskNotes: Graded pin-fins hold SiC junction <175 C at 350 kW fast-charge and sustained 30-min hill-climb. AM porosity controlled to <0.3% to avoid coolant weep; CuCr1Zr inserts handle peak-flux dies. Glycol erosion of fins validated over 15k cycles. Lower pressure drop reduces pump NVH. Premium BEV high-load thermal headroom preserved with ~8 K junction margin gain.

evidenceSources.title: AM pin-fin cold plate thermal data

title: Rotor shaft oil cooling via spray-bore replaces magnet derating
description: [800V EDU] Routes ATF through a hollow rotor shaft bore to cool magnets from the inside, replacing thermal derating margins and allowing reduced rare-earth content at continuous high load.

ideaData.title: Rotor shaft oil cooling via spray-bore replaces magnet derating
ideaData.technicalDescription: BEV applicability: 800-V EDU. Baseline derates the IPM rotor to keep NdFeB magnets below demag temperature, oversizing magnet mass. The change feeds ATF (~2.5 L/min) through a hollow shaft bore with radial spray holes, removing ~1.5 kW rotor loss and dropping magnet ΔT ~20 K, permitting a lower-grade magnet (N42UH vs N48UH) or reduced Dy/Tb. Alternative materials: shaft in 4340 steel vs 16MnCr5 case-hardened vs Ti-6Al-4V for mass; oil-feed coupling seals in FKM vs PTFE lip seals; exchanger plates 3003 brazed Al.
ideaData.materialGrade: 16MnCr5 hollow shaft / N42UH magnet / FKM seal
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: Gun-drilling plus cross-drilling of the shaft replaces solid-shaft turning; alternatives are friction-welded tube assembly or AM shaft. Adds rotary oil union and scavenge path, but reduces magnet mass and removes derate-driven lamination stack length. Net assembly neutral; balance step revalidated.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: Internal oil cooling cuts magnet grade and rare-earth cost.

ideaData.riskNotes: Internal cooling keeps magnets below demagnetisation knee during sustained off-road torque and trailer tow; lower-grade magnet validated against worst-case flux-weakening. Rotary union seal durability validated 15k h with FKM. Oil-feed dynamic balance preserved. No fast-charge impact (motor idle). NVH: oil flow through bore is inaudible versus gear mesh. Premium BEV continuous-torque performance preserved with magnet-cost reduction.

title: Oil-to-coolant brazed-plate exchanger replaces tube-and-fin unit
description: [800V EDU] Swaps the EDU oil-to-coolant tube-and-fin heat exchanger for a compact brazed-plate 3003 Al stack, cutting mass and footprint while improving oil heat rejection on continuous load.

ideaData.title: Oil-to-coolant brazed-plate exchanger replaces tube-and-fin unit
ideaData.technicalDescription: BEV applicability: 800-V EDU. Baseline uses a bulky tube-and-fin oil cooler rejecting ~5 kW with ~45 kPa oil-side drop. The change adopts a 3003 brazed-plate exchanger with chevron plates raising oil-side heat-transfer coefficient ~40%, rejecting the same 5 kW in ~35% less volume at ~30 kPa drop, with coolant 8 L/min and oil 4 L/min. Alternative materials: 3003/4004-clad brazed Al vs CuNi 90/10 plates vs 316L stainless for aggressive ATF; gaskets EPDM vs FKM; AlN not required.
ideaData.materialGrade: 3003/4004-clad brazed Al / CuNi 90/10 / 316L
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: Vacuum or CAB brazing of stacked stamped plates replaces tube bending and fin assembly; alternatives are diffusion-bonded plates or AM. Reduces part count ~30%, removes header welds, cuts footprint enabling EDU package shrink. Single braze cycle, one leak-test, lower BOM.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: Compact brazed-plate stack cuts mass and oil-side ΔT.

ideaData.riskNotes: Brazed-plate stack holds oil inlet below 110 C on sustained grade and tow loads, protecting gear-set viscosity and bearing film. 3003/4004 clad resists glycol and ATF corrosion validated 15-yr. Plate fatigue under pressure pulsation validated 20k cycles. No fast-charge interaction. NVH negligible. Premium BEV thermal rejection capacity preserved with margin while shrinking package and mass ~0.6 kg.

title: Skived-fin copper insert under SiC cuts junction ΔT
description: [800V EDU] Bonds a skived CuCr1Zr fin insert into the aluminium cold plate directly beneath the SiC module, spreading peak heat flux and lowering junction temperature without enlarging the cooler.

ideaData.title: Skived-fin copper insert under SiC cuts junction ΔT
ideaData.technicalDescription: BEV applicability: 800-V EDU / thermal interface. Baseline all-aluminium 6061 cold plate spreads ~150 W/cm2 SiC flux with ΔT ~40 K. The change inlays a skived CuCr1Zr (k~320 W/mK) fin insert under the die, raising local spreading and dropping junction-to-coolant ΔT ~9 K at 9 L/min water-glycol, ~28 kPa. Alternative materials: CuCr1Zr vs Cu-Ni vs pure-Cu insert; surrounding plate 6061-T6 vs 3003 brazed Al; TIM AlN ceramic vs sintered-silver vs graphite foil under module.
ideaData.materialGrade: CuCr1Zr skived insert / 6061-T6 Al / sintered-Ag
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: Skiving plus friction-stir or braze bonding of the Cu insert into the Al plate; alternatives are roll-bonding Cu strip or AM bimetal. Adds one bonded joint but avoids full-copper cost and mass. Bimetal interface leak-tested once; tooling moderate; BOM rises slightly, offset by smaller cooler.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: Local copper spreading lowers junction ΔT cost-effectively.

ideaData.riskNotes: Copper insert keeps SiC junction <175 C at 350 kW fast-charge and continuous high-load; bimetal CTE mismatch managed by friction-stir interface validated 15k thermal cycles. Sintered-silver TIM resists pump-out versus silicone. Glycol corrosion of Cu mitigated by inhibitor and Al galvanic isolation. No NVH impact. Premium BEV peak-power and sustained-load thermal margins preserved with ~9 K junction headroom.

evidenceSources.title: Cu insert cold plate thermal study

title: Integrated EDU housing cooling jacket cast in HPDC end-cover
description: [800V EDU] Casts the stator water jacket directly into the AlSi10MnMg HPDC housing, deleting a separate jacket sleeve, two O-ring joints and assembly steps while preserving cooling performance.

ideaData.title: Integrated EDU housing cooling jacket cast in HPDC end-cover
ideaData.technicalDescription: BEV applicability: 800-V EDU. Baseline presses a separate spiral jacket sleeve into the motor housing with two O-ring sealed interfaces. The change casts the helical coolant channel into the HPDC housing via lost-core or machined-and-capped channel, carrying 10 L/min water-glycol at ~30 kPa and holding stator ΔT within 3 K of baseline. Alternative materials: AlSi10MnMg HPDC vs A356-T6 gravity cast vs 6082-T6 extruded sleeve; channel cap in friction-stir-welded 6061 vs brazed 3003; seals EPDM vs FKM.
ideaData.materialGrade: AlSi10MnMg HPDC / A356-T6 / 6061 FSW cap
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: HPDC with salt/sand lost-core or CNC channel plus FSW cap replaces sleeve pressing; alternatives are gravity casting or extrude-and-insert. Deletes the sleeve, two O-rings and a press station; adds core or FSW step. Net joint count down two; single leak-test. Tooling rises, BOM and labour fall.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: Cast-in jacket deletes sleeve, seals and a press step.

ideaData.riskNotes: Cast-in jacket holds stator winding below 165 C on continuous off-road and tow loads; lost-core porosity controlled to prevent coolant-to-cavity weep, protecting dielectric integrity. FSW cap fatigue validated 15k thermal cycles and glycol exposure. No fast-charge interaction (motor side). NVH unaffected. Premium BEV continuous-power cooling preserved while reducing leak paths and warranty exposure.

title: Common cold-plate platform shared across front and rear inverters
description: [800V EDU] Standardises one cold-plate design across dual-motor front and rear SiC inverters, commonising tooling, seals and leak-test fixtures while meeting both heat loads through flow scaling.

ideaData.title: Common cold-plate platform shared across front and rear inverters
ideaData.technicalDescription: BEV applicability: 800-V EDU. Baseline uses two distinct cold-plate designs for front (lower power) and rear (higher power) inverters. The change defines a single 6061/3003 cold-plate footprint sized for the higher 14 kW rear load and scales front-axle cooling by reducing flow to 6 L/min (vs 9 L/min rear), holding both junction temperatures in spec. Alternative materials: 6061-T6 vs 3003 brazed Al vs AlSi10MnMg HPDC plate; TIM AlN vs graphite foil; ports common O-ring EPDM.
ideaData.materialGrade: 6061-T6 Al / 3003 brazed Al / AlN ceramic TIM
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: One brazing or roll-bond tool serves both axles; alternatives are extrusion or HPDC. Commonises stamping dies, braze fixtures and leak-test rigs across two part numbers into one, halving tooling spend and reducing spares. Slight over-capability on front axle accepted for commonisation BOM gain.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: Single cold-plate platform halves tooling across both axles.

ideaData.riskNotes: Common plate sized to rear 350 kW fast-charge and continuous towing/off-road load guarantees front-axle junction margin with surplus. Flow-scaled front loop avoids over-pumping NVH. Glycol corrosion and braze fatigue validated 15k cycles on the worst-case duty. Commonisation reduces validation duplication and warranty variants. Premium BEV dual-motor thermal performance preserved on both axles with margin.

title: Switch from oil to water-glycol motor jacket on lower-power axle
description: [800V EDU] Replaces oil-jacket cooling with a simpler water-glycol jacket on the lower-power front motor, removing scavenge pump and oil heat exchanger where continuous load allows.

ideaData.title: Switch from oil to water-glycol motor jacket on lower-power axle
ideaData.technicalDescription: BEV applicability: 800-V EDU. Baseline front motor uses oil-jacket plus spray cooling with scavenge pump and oil-to-coolant exchanger. On the lower continuous-rated front axle, the change reverts to a water-glycol jacket (10 L/min, ~28 kPa) holding stator below 160 C, deleting the oil pump, exchanger and oil volume. Alternative materials: jacket in 6082-T6 extruded vs AlSi10MnMg HPDC vs 3003 brazed; coolant 50/50 ethylene-glycol vs propylene-glycol vs OAT-inhibited; seals EPDM vs FKM.
ideaData.materialGrade: 6082-T6 Al jacket / EG 50/50 / EPDM seal
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: Extrusion or HPDC of a water jacket replaces oil-circuit machining; alternatives are roll-bond or cast-in channel. Deletes scavenge pump, oil exchanger, oil-fill station and two oil joints. Adds nothing net; reduces BOM, fluids logistics and leak-test scope to one circuit.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: Water jacket on light-duty axle deletes the oil sub-system.

ideaData.riskNotes: Applied only where front-axle continuous duty stays within water-jacket capability; verified that sustained off-road and tow modes shift torque rearward, keeping front stator below 160 C. Removing oil eliminates churning loss and a leak source. No fast-charge impact. NVH improves (one fewer pump). Premium BEV performance preserved by retaining oil cooling on the high-power rear axle.

evidenceSources.title: Water vs oil motor jacket trade study

title: Shared chiller and refrigerant valve block for pack and EDU
description: [Combined Loop] Consolidates pack and EDU active chilling onto one refrigerant chiller and integrated expansion-valve block, deleting a duplicate chiller and refrigerant lines while enabling demand-based cooling.

ideaData.title: Shared chiller and refrigerant valve block for pack and EDU
ideaData.technicalDescription: BEV applicability: combined loop / refrigerant. Baseline uses separate chillers for pack and EDU coolant. The change runs one R1234yf chiller (~6 kW) with an integrated electronic-expansion-valve block distributing cooling between domains on demand, supporting 800V 350 kW fast-charge pack chilling and high-load EDU rejection without both peaking simultaneously. Alternative materials: valve-block body AlSi10MnMg HPDC vs 6082-T6 extruded vs 316L manifold; chiller plates 3003 brazed Al vs CuNi; seals FKM vs HNBR for refrigerant.
ideaData.materialGrade: AlSi10MnMg HPDC block / 3003 brazed Al / HNBR
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: HPDC plus CNC port finishing for the EXV block replaces two separate chiller mounts and line sets; alternatives are extruded-and-welded or brazed manifold. Deletes one chiller, ~2 m refrigerant line, two service ports. Consolidates refrigerant evacuation/charge into one station; tooling up, BOM down.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: One chiller serves both domains via demand-based EXV block.

ideaData.riskNotes: Demand-based allocation verified that pack fast-charge chilling and peak EDU load rarely coincide; worst-case simultaneous demand handled by transient pack thermal mass buffering. Refrigerant isolation prevents cross-domain fault propagation. HNBR seals and braze joints validated 15-yr. Fast-charge 10-80% time preserved. NVH: single compressor lowers noise sources. Premium BEV thermal performance preserved with active-chill capacity margin.

title: Friction-stir-welded cold plate lid replaces vacuum brazing
description: [800V EDU] Closes the inverter cold-plate channel with a friction-stir-welded 6061 lid instead of vacuum brazing, cutting furnace energy, flatness rework and leak rejects on the SiC cooler.

ideaData.title: Friction-stir-welded cold plate lid replaces vacuum brazing
ideaData.technicalDescription: BEV applicability: 800-V EDU. Baseline vacuum-brazes a finned cold-plate assembly, incurring furnace cost and post-braze flatness distortion needing re-machining. The change machines channels in a 6061-T6 base and seals with an FSW 6061 lid, holding flatness <50 um for TIM contact and channel integrity at 9 L/min, ~28 kPa. Alternative materials: 6061-T6 base/lid vs 6082-T6 vs 3003; TIM AlN ceramic vs graphite foil vs silicone gap-filler; weld-zone treated for glycol corrosion.
ideaData.materialGrade: 6061-T6 base+lid / AlN ceramic TIM / 6082-T6 alt
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: Friction-stir welding replaces vacuum/CAB brazing; alternatives are laser welding or roll-bonding. Removes furnace cycle and flux, cuts flatness rework ~70%, lowers leak rejects from braze voids. Adds FSW station and post-weld flatness check. Net tooling neutral, scrap and energy fall.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: FSW lid removes brazing furnace cost and flatness rework.

ideaData.riskNotes: FSW joint holds channel pressure to 3x peak with no braze voids, improving leak yield; flatness under 50 um preserves TIM ΔT for SiC junction <175 C at 350 kW and continuous load. Weld-zone glycol corrosion mitigated by alloy choice and inhibitor, validated 15k cycles. No NVH impact. Premium BEV thermal performance preserved with improved manufacturing robustness and lower scrap.

evidenceSources.title: FSW vs braze cold plate cost

title: Integrated gearbox oil cooling using EDU housing fins
description: [800V EDU] Adds cast cooling fins and an oil sump channel to the EDU housing to reject gearbox heat passively, downsizing or deleting a dedicated oil cooler on moderate-duty axles.

ideaData.title: Integrated gearbox oil cooling using EDU housing fins
ideaData.technicalDescription: BEV applicability: 800-V EDU. Baseline uses a dedicated oil-to-coolant exchanger for the gearbox even on moderate-duty axles. The change casts external fins and an integrated sump cooling channel into the AlSi10MnMg housing, rejecting ~2 kW convectively and via coolant cross-flow, allowing a smaller exchanger or deletion where duty permits, holding oil below 110 C. Alternative materials: AlSi10MnMg HPDC vs A356-T6 vs 6082-T6; fin geometry cast vs CNC; coolant cross-channel cap brazed 3003 vs FSW 6061.
ideaData.materialGrade: AlSi10MnMg HPDC finned housing / A356-T6 / 3003 cap
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: Casting external fins and sump channel into the HPDC housing replaces a bolt-on exchanger and bracket; alternatives are gravity cast or extruded fin add-on. Deletes exchanger, bracket and two oil joints on moderate-duty axles. Adds casting complexity; BOM and assembly fall, single oil circuit leak-test.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: Cast housing fins reject gearbox heat, downsizing the cooler.

ideaData.riskNotes: Applied only where gearbox continuous load keeps oil below 110 C with cast-fin rejection; sustained tow/off-road duty retains a downsized exchanger as backstop. Fin-cast porosity controlled to maintain housing structural and sealing integrity. Oil viscosity and bearing film protected. No fast-charge interaction. NVH unaffected. Premium BEV durability preserved with thermal backstop for peak duty.

title: Salt-hydrate PCM buffer caps SiC junction during fast-charge
description: [800V EDU] Adds a thin salt-hydrate PCM layer adjacent to the SiC module to absorb transient heat spikes during 800V fast-charge boost and off-road peaks, smoothing junction temperature.

ideaData.title: Salt-hydrate PCM buffer caps SiC junction during fast-charge
ideaData.technicalDescription: BEV applicability: 800-V EDU / thermal interface.
