Baseline relies solely on coolant transient response, allowing junction overshoot during brief 350 kW boost and rock-crawl peaks. The change places an encapsulated salt-hydrate PCM (melt ~58 C, latent ~250 kJ/kg) layer near the module to absorb ~30 kJ transient, capping junction overshoot ~8 K and reducing required peak coolant flow. Alternative materials: salt-hydrate vs paraffin PCM vs metallic PCM (Bi-Sn); encapsulation 3003 Al vs graphite-foam composite; base TIM AlN vs sintered-silver.
ideaData.materialGrade: Salt-hydrate PCM / paraffin alt / 3003 Al capsule
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: Encapsulating PCM in a stamped 3003 Al pocket bonded to the cold plate; alternatives are graphite-foam PCM composite or AM capsule. Adds one sub-assembly and a fill/seal step but lets the cold plate and pump be sized to average, not peak, load, reducing cooler and pump BOM. One added leak/seal check.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: PCM buffer lets cooler and pump size to average not peak.

ideaData.riskNotes: PCM caps SiC junction <175 C during 350 kW boost and off-road peaks, enabling pump downsizing without sacrificing margin. Salt-hydrate phase-segregation and supercooling mitigated by nucleators and thickeners, validated 15k cycles. Encapsulation prevents leakage near power electronics (dielectric safety). NVH improves via smaller pump. Premium BEV transient performance preserved with smoothed thermal profile.

evidenceSources.title: PCM thermal buffer for power module

title: Extruded multi-port stator jacket replaces machined spiral channel
description: [800V EDU] Forms the stator cooling jacket from a rolled multi-port aluminium extrusion instead of a machined spiral, cutting machining time and improving channel uniformity for even stator cooling.

ideaData.title: Extruded multi-port stator jacket replaces machined spiral channel
ideaData.technicalDescription: BEV applicability: 800-V EDU. Baseline machines a spiral coolant groove into the housing, slow and prone to uneven wall thickness. The change rolls a 6082-T6 multi-port micro-channel extrusion into a cylindrical jacket, giving uniform parallel channels (Dh ~2.5 mm) at 10 L/min, ~30 kPa, improving stator circumferential ΔT uniformity ~3 K. Alternative materials: 6082-T6 vs 6061-T6 vs 3003 extrusion; jacket-to-housing interface FSW vs braze vs shrink-fit; seals EPDM vs FKM.
ideaData.materialGrade: 6082-T6 MPE extrusion / 6061-T6 / 3003 alt
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: Multi-port extrusion plus roll-forming and FSW seam replaces 5-axis spiral machining; alternatives are roll-bond sleeve or HPDC cast jacket. Cuts machining hours ~65%, improves wall uniformity, removes deep-groove tooling. Adds roll-form and seam-weld steps; net tooling neutral, scrap and cycle time fall.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: Multi-port extrusion replaces slow spiral machining.

ideaData.riskNotes: Uniform parallel channels improve circumferential stator cooling, holding windings below 165 C on continuous off-road/tow loads with reduced hotspot scatter. FSW seam validated to 3x coolant pressure and 15k thermal cycles; glycol corrosion managed by alloy and inhibitor. No fast-charge interaction (motor side). NVH unchanged. Premium BEV continuous-power thermal uniformity preserved while cutting machining cost.

title: Graphite-foil TIM replaces silicone gap-filler under SiC module
description: [800V EDU] Replaces pumped-out silicone gap-filler under the SiC power module with a compressible graphite-foil TIM, cutting interface thermal resistance and rework while improving long-term junction stability.

ideaData.title: Graphite-foil TIM replaces silicone gap-filler under SiC module
ideaData.technicalDescription: BEV applicability: 800-V EDU / thermal interface. Baseline uses silicone gap-filler (k~3 W/mK) prone to pump-out and dry-out, raising interface ΔT over life. The change uses compressible graphite-foil TIM (k~12 W/mK in-plane, ~5 through) at ~150 W/cm2, dropping interface ΔT ~5 K and resisting pump-out under thermal cycling. Alternative materials: graphite foil vs AlN ceramic vs sintered-silver vs phase-change TIM; cold-plate 6061-T6 vs 3003; surface finish controlled <Ra 0.8.
ideaData.materialGrade: Graphite-foil TIM / AlN ceramic / sintered-Ag
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: Die-cut graphite-foil placement replaces dispensed silicone; alternatives are screen-printed sinter-paste or ceramic shim. Removes dispensing equipment and cure step, cuts rework from pump-out, enables dry assembly. Adds pick-place of foil; BOM and warranty (interface degradation) fall, leak-test unaffected.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: Graphite foil cuts interface ΔT and life-time pump-out.

ideaData.riskNotes: Graphite foil holds SiC junction <175 C at 350 kW fast-charge and continuous load with stable interface ΔT over 15k thermal cycles, eliminating silicone dry-out warranty risk. Electrical isolation maintained where required by ceramic-backed variant (dielectric safety). Compression set controlled by clamp design. No NVH impact. Premium BEV long-term thermal stability preserved versus degrading silicone.

title: Single coolant pump with smart valve replaces dual-pump EDU loop
description: [Pumping] Consolidates two EDU coolant pumps into one variable-speed pump and proportional valve, matching flow to motor and inverter demand separately while cutting pump count and parasitic draw.

ideaData.title: Single coolant pump with smart valve replaces dual-pump EDU loop
ideaData.technicalDescription: BEV applicability: 800-V EDU / pumping. Baseline runs separate pumps for inverter cold plate and motor jacket. The change uses one variable-speed pump (~30 W peak) with a proportional valve splitting flow (e.g. 9 L/min inverter, 10 L/min motor) by demand, cutting parasitic energy ~25% at part load and deleting a pump. Alternative materials: pump housing PA66-GF35 vs AlSi10MnMg vs 6082-T6; valve body POM vs PPS-GF40; impeller PPS vs brass; seals EPDM vs FKM.
ideaData.materialGrade: PA66-GF35 pump / PPS-GF40 valve / EPDM seal
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: Injection-moulded valve body and single pump replace two pump assemblies; alternatives are HPDC valve block or extruded manifold. Deletes one pump, one connector pair and ~0.8 m hose. Adds valve and controller logic; net BOM down, one fewer leak-test branch and assembly station.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: One smart-valved pump replaces two, cutting parasitic draw.

ideaData.riskNotes: Single-pump architecture maintains independent inverter and motor flow via proportional valve, holding SiC junction <175 C and stator <165 C across continuous off-road/tow load. Limp-home valve default ensures redundancy on valve fault. Lower parasitic draw improves range; reduced pump count cuts NVH sources. Fast-charge preconditioning flow preserved. Premium BEV thermal independence and performance preserved.

evidenceSources.title: Single vs dual pump EDU study

title: Commonised rotary multi-way valve across BEV thermal variants
description: [Combined Loop] Standardises one rotary multi-way coolant valve across single- and dual-motor platform variants, commonising the valve, actuator and seals to cut part numbers and validation spend.

ideaData.title: Commonised rotary multi-way valve across BEV thermal variants
ideaData.technicalDescription: BEV applicability: combined loop. Baseline uses different valve assemblies per drivetrain variant. The change defines one 8-port rotary valve with software-configured port maps serving single- and dual-motor combined battery+EDU loops at up to 14 L/min, ~25 kPa. Alternative materials: valve body PPS-GF40 vs PA66-GF50 vs AlSi10MnMg; rotor POM vs PEEK; seals FKM vs EPDM; actuator gear PA66-GF30 vs PBT.
ideaData.materialGrade: PPS-GF40 valve body / PEEK rotor / FKM seal
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: One injection-mould tool and actuator serve all variants; alternatives are HPDC body or modular plate valve. Commonises mould, actuator and leak-test fixture across 3+ part numbers into one, cutting tooling and spares. Software port maps replace hardware variants; BOM and validation duplication fall.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: One software-configured valve replaces multiple variant valves.

ideaData.riskNotes: Single valve sized to dual-motor worst-case flow guarantees single-motor margin; software port maps validated for all routing states including fast-charge pack chill and high-load EDU rejection. Sealing across ports prevents cross-domain leakage. FKM and PEEK durability validated 15-yr/glycol. NVH from actuator controlled by gear damping. Premium BEV thermal flexibility preserved with reduced variant complexity.

title: HPDC inverter housing integrates cold plate and busbar mount
description: [800V EDU] Casts the SiC inverter cold plate, coolant ports and busbar mounting into one AlSi10MnMg HPDC housing, deleting a separate cold plate, fasteners and a sealed joint while preserving junction cooling.

ideaData.title: HPDC inverter housing integrates cold plate and busbar mount
ideaData.technicalDescription: BEV applicability: 800-V EDU. Baseline bolts a discrete cold plate to the inverter housing with a sealed coolant interface and many fasteners. The change casts the channel, ports and busbar bosses into one AlSi10MnMg HPDC housing with machined-and-FSW-capped channels, holding SiC junction <175 C at 9 L/min, ~28 kPa. Alternative materials: AlSi10MnMg HPDC vs A356-T6 vs 6082-T6 fabricated; TIM AlN ceramic vs sintered-silver; channel cap FSW 6061 vs braze 3003.
ideaData.materialGrade: AlSi10MnMg HPDC / AlN ceramic TIM / 6061 FSW cap
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: HPDC with machined channel and FSW cap replaces a bolt-on brazed cold plate; alternatives are A356 gravity cast or fabricated plate. Deletes ~8 fasteners, a gasketed joint and a bracket. Adds casting and FSW steps; net joint count and assembly stations fall, single leak-test, tooling up.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: Cast-in cold plate deletes bolt-on plate, gasket and fasteners.

ideaData.riskNotes: Integrated cast cold plate holds SiC junction <175 C at 350 kW fast-charge and sustained high-load; FSW-capped channel validated to 3x pressure and 15k thermal cycles. Casting porosity controlled to prevent coolant-to-cavity weep near HV busbars (dielectric/creepage safety). Glycol corrosion mitigated by alloy and inhibitor. No NVH impact. Premium BEV thermal and electrical performance preserved with fewer leak paths.

title: Propylene-glycol switch with optimised inhibitor cuts coolant cost
description: [Combined Loop] Switches the shared battery+EDU loop to an optimised propylene-glycol OAT coolant, improving safety and lifetime while reducing service and corrosion-warranty cost across the combined circuit.

ideaData.title: Propylene-glycol switch with optimised inhibitor cuts coolant cost
ideaData.technicalDescription: BEV applicability: combined loop. Baseline uses ethylene-glycol coolant needing more frequent service and posing toxicity handling cost. The change adopts an optimised propylene-glycol (50/50) OAT-inhibited coolant in the shared battery+EDU loop, holding pump/flow at 12 L/min with ~7% higher viscosity offset by channel sizing, extending service interval and reducing corrosion. Alternative materials: PG-OAT vs EG-OAT vs hybrid HOAT; aluminium 3003/6082 wetted parts; seals EPDM vs FKM; nitrite-free inhibitor package.
ideaData.materialGrade: PG-OAT 50/50 / EG-OAT alt / 3003+6082 wetted Al
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: Coolant change is fill-spec only; no new tooling. Alternatives are HOAT or nitrite-OAT packages. Impacts BOM via lower lifetime fluid cost and reduced corrosion-driven warranty; assembly unchanged; one fill-station spec update. Validates inhibitor compatibility across mixed Al/Cu/elastomer wetted set.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: Optimised PG-OAT cuts service, corrosion and toxicity cost.

ideaData.riskNotes: PG-OAT maintains pack and EDU cooling within spec; ~7% viscosity rise offset by channel sizing to preserve fast-charge pack chilling and high-load EDU flow. Lower toxicity improves service safety; OAT inhibitor protects mixed Al/Cu wetted parts against glycol corrosion, validated 15-yr. EPDM compatibility confirmed. Negligible NVH/pump impact. Premium BEV thermal performance preserved with longer fluid life and reduced warranty exposure.

title: Stator end-winding direct oil cooling enables shorter lamination stack
description: [800V EDU] Direct oil cooling of end-windings lifts copper current density, allowing a shorter lamination stack and less copper and steel for the same continuous torque, cutting material and weight.

ideaData.title: Stator end-winding direct oil cooling enables shorter lamination stack
ideaData.technicalDescription: BEV applicability: 800-V EDU. Baseline sizes the lamination stack and copper for jacket-limited thermal capacity. Direct ATF cooling of end-windings (4 L/min, ~7 cSt) removes the hotspot constraint, enabling ~10% higher RMS current density and a ~8% shorter stack for equal continuous torque, cutting electrical steel and copper mass ~1.2 kg. Alternative materials: lamination M250-35A vs M270-35A vs cobalt-iron for high-flux; conductor Cu vs hairpin-Cu vs Al; oil-feed manifold AlSi10MnMg HPDC vs PA66-GF35.
ideaData.materialGrade: M250-35A lamination / hairpin-Cu / AlSi10MnMg manifold
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: Direct-cool manifold via HPDC or extrusion enables stack shortening in stamping; alternatives are AM manifold or stamped spray ring. Reduces lamination stamping count and copper length, lowering material BOM and mass; adds oil manifold and scavenge path. Net assembly neutral; leak-test extends to oil circuit.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: Direct oil cooling shortens stack, cutting steel and copper.

ideaData.riskNotes: Direct end-winding cooling keeps insulation below 180 C at the higher current density on continuous off-road and tow duty, validated against demag and thermal-aging limits. Dielectric ATF avoids partial discharge. Shorter stack revalidated for NVH (cogging/torque ripple) and rotordynamics. No fast-charge interaction (motor side). Scavenge-pump whine isolated. Premium BEV continuous-torque density preserved while cutting mass and material cost.

evidenceSources.title: Direct end-winding oil cooling stack reduction

title: Rotor sleeve cooling channel replaces magnet eddy derating
description: [800V EDU] Adds a thin oil-fed cooling sleeve around the rotor to remove eddy-loss heat, replacing eddy-driven magnet derating and enabling segmented-magnet cost savings at 800V high-frequency operation.

ideaData.title: Rotor sleeve cooling channel replaces magnet eddy derating
ideaData.technicalDescription: BEV applicability: 800-V EDU. Baseline derates the rotor against eddy-current heating from 800V high-switching-frequency harmonics, oversizing magnet and segmentation. The change adds an oil-fed sleeve (2.5 L/min ATF) removing ~1.2 kW eddy heat, dropping magnet ΔT ~18 K and permitting coarser magnet segmentation (fewer segments) and lower-grade Dy content. Alternative materials: sleeve in Ti-6Al-4V vs 316L vs CFRP wrap; magnet N42UH vs N48UH segmented; oil-union seal FKM vs PTFE.
ideaData.materialGrade: Ti-6Al-4V sleeve / N42UH segmented magnet / FKM
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: Sleeve formed by deep-draw or roll-form plus laser seam; alternatives are CFRP filament-wind or machined ring. Adds sleeve and oil feed but removes fine magnet segmentation steps and derate-driven mass. Net assembly slightly up, magnet processing cost down; balance and leak-test revalidated.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: Sleeve oil cooling cuts magnet segmentation and Dy content.

ideaData.riskNotes: Sleeve cooling holds magnets below demag knee under 800V high-frequency continuous operation and off-road peaks; coarser segmentation validated against eddy-loss and demag with cooling margin. Ti sleeve fatigue and oil-union sealing validated 15k h. Rotordynamics and balance preserved. No fast-charge interaction (motor side). NVH unaffected by oil flow. Premium BEV high-speed performance preserved with magnet-cost reduction.

evidenceSources.title: Rotor cooling sleeve for high-frequency drive

title: Integrated thermal circuit deletes redundant degas and reservoir
description: [Combined Loop] Merges battery and EDU degas bottles and expansion reservoirs into one shared component in the integrated thermal circuit, deleting a bottle, hoses and fill steps across the combined loop.

ideaData.title: Integrated thermal circuit deletes redundant degas and reservoir
ideaData.technicalDescription: BEV applicability: combined loop. Baseline has separate degas/expansion reservoirs for battery and EDU loops. The change uses one shared blow-moulded reservoir with internal baffling serving both, sized to total coolant expansion (~1.2 L) and degas needs at 12 L/min combined flow. Alternative materials: reservoir in HDPE blow-mould vs PA6-GF vs PP; level sensor capacitive vs float; hose EPDM vs FKM-lined; cap with integrated pressure relief.
ideaData.materialGrade: HDPE blow-mould reservoir / PA6-GF alt / EPDM hose
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: Blow-moulding one shared reservoir replaces two; alternatives are injection-moulded two-shell or rotomould. Deletes one bottle, ~1 m hose, two clamps and a fill port. Consolidates fill/bleed into one station; tooling for one larger bottle, BOM and assembly stations fall, single leak/bleed check.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: Shared reservoir deletes a degas bottle, hoses and fill step.

ideaData.riskNotes: Shared reservoir sized for worst-case combined expansion and degas during fast-charge pack chill and high-load EDU operation, with baffling preventing aeration; verified no cross-domain coolant migration affecting isolation. Pressure-relief cap protects against over-pressure. EPDM/HDPE glycol durability validated 15-yr. NVH unaffected. Premium BEV thermal reliability preserved while reducing leak paths and warranty exposure.

title: AlN ceramic substrate cooling enables smaller SiC die count
description: [800V EDU] Upgrades the SiC module substrate to AlN active-metal-braze with optimised cold-plate coupling, lowering thermal resistance enough to reduce paralleled die count, cutting expensive SiC area.

ideaData.title: AlN ceramic substrate cooling enables smaller SiC die count
ideaData.technicalDescription: BEV applicability: 800-V EDU / thermal interface. Baseline uses Al2O3 DBC substrate forcing extra paralleled SiC dies to limit junction temperature. The change adopts AlN AMB substrate (k~170 vs ~24 W/mK for Al2O3) with a graded pin-fin cold plate, cutting junction-to-coolant resistance ~25% at ~150 W/cm2, enabling ~10% fewer SiC dies for the same current. Alternative materials: AlN AMB vs Si3N4 AMB vs Al2O3 DBC; baseplate Cu vs AlSiC; TIM sintered-silver vs AlN shim; cold plate AlSi10Mg AM.
ideaData.materialGrade: AlN AMB substrate / Si3N4 AMB / sintered-Ag TIM
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: AlN active-metal-braze substrate with sintered-silver die attach replaces Al2O3 DBC with solder; alternatives are Si3N4 AMB or AlSiC baseplate. Higher substrate cost offset by removing paralleled dies and shrinking module footprint. Sintering line replaces reflow; net die-area BOM falls sharply, assembly comparable.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: Higher-k substrate cuts costly SiC die count for same current.

ideaData.riskNotes: AlN AMB and sintered-silver attach hold SiC junction <175 C at 350 kW fast-charge and continuous high-load with fewer dies, validated for power-cycling >1M cycles (sintered-Ag far exceeds solder fatigue). Si3N4 option for higher mechanical robustness. Dielectric isolation maintained per creepage spec (safety). No NVH impact. Premium BEV 800V power density preserved while cutting expensive SiC area and module mass.

evidenceSources.title: AlN AMB vs Al2O3 DBC thermal data
AlN substrate SiC module die reduction

title: Off-road sustained-load thermal mode with active flow boost
description: [Combined Loop] Adds a software-driven off-road thermal mode that boosts pump flow and reallocates chiller capacity to EDU during sustained low-speed high-torque duty, avoiding hardware over-sizing.

ideaData.title: Off-road sustained-load thermal mode with active flow boost
ideaData.technicalDescription: BEV applicability: combined loop / pumping. Baseline over-sizes EDU cooling hardware to survive worst-case off-road crawl where airflow is low and torque high. The change adds a control mode boosting the shared pump to 14 L/min and reallocating chiller duty to EDU during sustained rock-crawl/tow, holding SiC junction <175 C and stator <165 C without larger radiators or pumps. Alternative materials: control only; valve PPS-GF40; pump PA66-GF35; no new wetted material; relies on existing AlN TIM and 3003 cold plate.
ideaData.materialGrade: Software + PPS-GF40 valve / existing 3003 cold plate / AlN TIM
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: Calibration and control change with existing hardware; alternatives are larger radiator or added auxiliary pump (both rejected). No new tooling; deletes the need for radiator/pump up-sizing on off-road variants. BOM unchanged or reduced versus hardware over-size; validation via thermal soak testing.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: Software thermal mode avoids hardware over-sizing for off-road.

ideaData.riskNotes: Off-road mode verified to hold SiC junction <175 C and stator <165 C during 30-min sustained low-airflow crawl and trailer tow by boosting flow and reallocating chiller from pack (which is at low load then). Pack thermal margin maintained. Avoids radiator/pump over-size, saving mass and cost. Flow boost NVH bounded by speed limit. Fast-charge mode unaffected. Premium BEV off-road durability preserved without hardware penalty.
